mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, while also potentially leading to higher discount rates. This combination drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which directly impacts consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic activity, signaling reduced corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales reflects strong consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy stance by the central bank, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve policy impacts market rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the average return during such periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous sharp decline in housing starts and a significant rise in the unemployment rate signals a severe economic downturn, as both consumer confidence and investment collapse, leading to strong negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% in 3 months AND UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and labor markets are interconnected
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 30% of triggered instances, or the average return during such periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods where PAYEMS growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in real GDP growth signals a broad economic slowdown, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 0% (quarter-over-quarter annualized)
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative.
